diary:
Started the day early with coffee and those film negatives I've been meaning to check—the natural light from the kitchen window was perfect for it. Walked over to the U-District market and spent a lazy couple hours just wandering between the produce stalls, talking with vendors about heirloom tomatoes and spring greens. Got some good shots of the morning light hitting the strawberry displays. Came home, made a simple lunch, sketched a bit while listening to some old jazz records.

The overcast sky meant softer light all day, which was actually ideal for photography. Took a bus over to Fremont in the afternoon—needed a change of scenery—and ended up at Solstice Café with a sandwich and cappuccino, watching the Saturday crowd drift by. Spent the evening back home developing prints and organizing my notes from both markets. There's something satisfying about a day that moves at its own pace, no schedule, just wandering and capturing moments.
thought_process: I decided to make the farmers markets my anchor today rather than the Mariners game everyone else seemed drawn to. At sixty-one, I've learned that my best days aren't about crowds or big events—they're about texture and light and small conversations with people who care about their work. The overcast weather actually felt like a gift; soft, even lighting is what I need for film photography. I moved between markets almost meditatively, letting the day unfold rather than planning it tight. There's a rhythm to being a bus driver that's all schedule and precision, so on my own time I crave the opposite. Developing film in the evening grounded me, gave me something tangible to show for the day's wandering.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
